title: Fixed log prob result generation

step_plan.summary: Fix non-streaming `/v1/completions` and Python engine completion aggregation so `CompletionLogProbs` is appended/merged across streamed chunks per choice, rather than overwritten, producing token-aligned logprob arrays for the full generated text.

steps.operation: Add a shared helper to merge/append per-token completion logprob arrays across streamed chunks
steps.reason: Centralize the correct semantics for accumulating `CompletionLogProbs` for the same `choice.index` across multiple chunks (append `tokens`, `token_logprobs`, `top_logprobs` instead of overwriting), so REST and Python engine paths can share identical behavior.
steps.side_effects: Any caller that starts using this helper will change behavior from “last chunk wins” to “append across chunks”; ensure it handles `None`/missing logprobs gracefully and preserves current `text_offset` behavior (likely stays `None`). May require updating/adding unit tests for multi-chunk completions with logprobs.

steps.operation: Use the shared merge helper in the async non-streaming completion aggregation loop
steps.reason: The async non-streaming completion path currently overwrites `logprob_results[choice.index]` per chunk; it must instead accumulate token-level arrays so the final response contains logprobs for all generated tokens.
steps.side_effects: Async Python client non-streaming completions will return longer `choices[i].logprobs.*` arrays matching the completion text length; downstream code/tests that assumed a single entry (buggy behavior) may need adjustment.

steps.operation: Use the same merge helper in the sync non-streaming completion aggregation loop
steps.reason: Keep sync engine behavior consistent with async engine and REST server: non-streaming completion responses should preserve logprobs across all chunks rather than only the last chunk’s token.
steps.side_effects: Synchronous Python API non-streaming completions will now include full-length logprobs arrays; may affect any consumers relying on the previous incorrect truncation.

steps.operation: Fix REST /v1/completions non-streaming aggregation to merge logprobs per choice instead of overwriting
steps.reason: The REST server builds a non-streaming response by consuming streamed chunks; it concatenates text correctly but currently overwrites `logprobs` per chunk. It must merge/append token-level logprobs for the same `choice.index` to match OpenAI schema expectations.
steps.side_effects: REST clients will observe correct multi-token `logprobs` output; ensure behavior when a chunk lacks logprobs (don’t break alignment, don’t raise). May require updating any golden snapshots/contract tests for `/v1/completions`.
